results.question: What are the main subsystems / top-level modules of this codebase? Describe the overall architecture.

answers.answer:
Project: codebase-memory-mcp ("cbm") — a C program that parses source repos into a queryable code knowledge graph. Entry point: src/main.c (main()). Architecture is a two-layer split — an outer application/orchestration layer under src/* and an inner parsing/extraction engine under internal/cbm/*. Graph has 283 files/modules grouped into 19 top-level folders.

MAIN SUBSYSTEMS / TOP-LEVEL MODULES:

Application layer (src/):
- src/pipeline/ — the core orchestration engine; a multi-pass pipeline. ~35 modules, each a "pass": pass_definitions, pass_calls, pass_usages, pass_complexity, pass_semantic / pass_semantic_edges, pass_similarity, pass_enrichment, pass_envscan, pass_infrascan, pass_k8s, pass_configures/configlink, pass_gitdiff, pass_githistory, pass_tests, pass_route_nodes, pass_pkgmap, pass_compile_commands, pass_cross_repo, pass_lsp_cross, pass_parallel; plus pipeline.h, pipeline_incremental.c, registry.c, fqn.c, worker_pool.h. Central types: cbm_pipeline, cbm_registry.
- src/foundation/ — base utilities/runtime: arena & slab allocators, mem.c, hash_table, dyn_array, str_intern/str_util, log, diagnostics, profile, platform/compat (compat.c, compat_fs, compat_regex, compat_thread, win_utf8), system_info, vmem, yaml. Types: CBMHashTable, CBMInternPool, cbm_arena.
- src/cli/ — command-line interface: cli.c, hook_augment.c, progress_sink.h. Type: cbm_config.
- src/mcp/ — MCP server interface (mcp.h/mcp.c). Types: cbm_mcp_server, cbm_pipeline, cbm_watcher.
- src/cypher/ — openCypher query engine (cypher.h). Types: cbm_query, cbm_expr.
- src/store/ + src/graph_buffer/ — graph persistence/buffering: store.c, graph_buffer.c. Types: cbm_store, cbm_gbuf.
- src/discover/ — repo discovery: file walking, gitignore.c, language.c, userconfig.c. Type: cbm_gitignore.
- src/semantic/ — semantic analysis / AST profiling: semantic.c, ast_profile.h. Type: cbm_sem_corpus.
- src/simhash/ — near-duplicate detection: minhash.h (LSH). Types: cbm_lsh_index.
- src/git/ — git integration (git_context.c).
- src/watcher/ — file-system watcher for incremental re-index (watcher.c). Type: cbm_watcher.
- src/traces/ — runtime trace ingestion (traces.h).
- src/ui/ — embedded HTTP server + 3D graph viewer: http_server.c, httpd.h, layout3d.h (octree), embedded_assets, config.c. Exposes REST routes: /api/index, /api/index-status, /api/adr, /api/processes, /api/process-kill. Types: cbm_http_server, cbm_httpd, cbm_http_conn.

Extraction engine (internal/cbm/):
- Tree-sitter grammar bindings — ~160 grammar_*.c modules (one per language: c, cpp, python, rust, go, java, swift, etc.), plus ts_runtime.c and lang_specs.c. This is the bulk of the file count.
- Extractors — extract_defs, extract_calls, extract_imports, extract_usages, extract_type_refs, extract_type_assigns, extract_semantic, extract_channels, extract_env_accesses, extract_k8s; shared via extract_unified.h, extract_node_stack.h, helpers.h. cbm_node_text is a top hotspot (fan-in 20).
- LSP-like symbol resolution (internal/cbm/lsp/) — per-language resolvers (c_lsp, go_lsp, py_lsp, rust_lsp, ts_lsp, java/kotlin/php/cs_lsp), a type system (type_rep.h CBMType, type_registry.h CBMTypeRegistry, scope.h CBMScope), rust_cargo/rust_proc_macros/rust_rustdoc, and generated/*_stdlib_data.c seed tables. cbm_registry_add_type/add_func and cbm_type_func/named/builtin are top hotspots (fan-in 17-20).
- Storage codecs — sqlite_writer.c (cbm_db_writer), zstd_store.c, lz4_store.c.
- Misc — ac.c (Aho-Corasick automaton CBMAutomaton), service_patterns.c, preprocessor.h.

GRAPH SIZE: 4595 nodes / 16924 edges. Functions 3082, Macros 539, Files 283, Modules 283, Fields 250, Variables 75, Classes/structs 56, Folders 19, Routes 6.

ARCHITECTURE HOTSPOTS (highest fan-in, i.e. the shared core utilities everything depends on): cbm_arena_alloc (27), cbm_registry_add_type (20), cbm_registry_add_func (20), cbm_arena_strndup (20), cbm_node_text (20), cbm_type_func (19), cbm_arena_sprintf (19), cbm_type_named (18), cbm_arena_strdup (18), cbm_type_builtin (17). The arena allocator (foundation) and the type/symbol registry (lsp) are the two architectural pillars all subsystems route through.

OVERALL FLOW: CLI/MCP/UI/watcher front-ends -> discover (find files) -> internal/cbm tree-sitter grammars + extractors + lsp type resolution parse each file -> src/pipeline multi-pass passes build/enrich graph nodes & edges (defs, calls, usages, semantic, similarity, infra/k8s/env, git history) -> graph_buffer/store + sqlite/zstd/lz4 codecs persist it -> cypher engine answers queries, served back via MCP/CLI/HTTP UI.

This is **codebase-memory-mcp-pro** — a C-language code-intelligence engine (~347 files, 4077 graph nodes / 14516 edges) that parses source repos via tree-sitter, builds a knowledge graph, and serves it over an MCP server + CLI. All symbols share the `cbm_` prefix. Architecture is a layered pipeline organized into these top-level modules (by source directory):

ENTRY / INTERFACE LAYER
- `src/main.c` — process entry point: `main()` (L387), `run_cli()` (L231), `handle_subcommand()` (L310), `run_corpus_phase()`.
- `src/cli/` — CLI commands: `cli.c` with `cbm_cmd_install/uninstall/update/config()` (install, update, agent-config detection), `hook_augment.c` (`cbm_cmd_hook_augment`).
- `src/mcp/mcp.c` — MCP server / JSON-RPC tool dispatch: `cbm_mcp_handle_tool()`, `handle_index_repository()`, `handle_delete_project()`, `maybe_auto_index()`, plus arg helpers `cbm_mcp_get_string/int/bool_arg()` (community 34).
- `src/ui/http_server.c` — embedded HTTP/web UI browser: `handle_browse()`, `db_path_for_project()` (community 1).

CORE EXTRACTION ENGINE
- `internal/cbm/cbm.c` — the central extraction context (`CBMExtractCtx`) and driver `cbm_extract_file()` (L499), `cbm_init()`, `cbm_alloc_init()`. This is the highest-betweenness bridge node connecting ~22 communities.
- `internal/cbm/lsp/` — language/type-resolution layer (LSP-style): type representation `type_rep.c` (`cbm_type_unknown/named/builtin/func/pointer/slice`), `type_registry.c` (`cbm_registry_lookup_type/func/method/symbol`), `scope.c` (`cbm_scope_push/pop/bind/lookup`), and per-language resolvers e.g. `go_lsp.c` (`cbm_run_go_lsp`, `GoLSPContext`) plus C/C#/TS/Python/Java/Rust/PHP/Kotlin LSP contexts (communities 5,6,8,9,10,11,23,25).
- `internal/cbm/lsp/generated/*_stdlib_data.c` — generated per-language stdlib/crate registries: `cbm_c_/cpp_/go_/rust_/csharp_/php_stdlib_register()` (communities 16, 21).
- `internal/cbm/helpers.c` — `cbm_node_text()` (the #1 god node, 220 edges) used by all extractors.

EXTRACTORS (per-feature passes, mostly community-per-concern)
- Distinct extractors each in their own community: `cbm_extract_definitions`, `cbm_extract_calls`, `cbm_extract_imports`, `cbm_extract_usages`, `cbm_extract_type_refs`, `cbm_extract_type_assigns`, `cbm_extract_semantic`, `cbm_extract_channels`, `cbm_extract_env_accesses`, `cbm_extract_k8s`, `cbm_extract_http_info`, `cbm_extract_flags`.

PIPELINE / ORCHESTRATION
- `src/pipeline/` — pipeline driver `pipeline.c` (`cbm_pipeline_t`, `run_parallel_pipeline()`, `run_sequential_pipeline()`, `run_extraction_phase()`), `pipeline_incremental.c` (`cbm_pipeline_run_incremental`, incremental re-index), `pass_parallel.c` (`cbm_parallel_extract/resolve`, worker threads), `fqn.c` / `pass_pkgmap.c` (fully-qualified-name + module resolution), and many `pass_*.c` passes: `pass_calls`, `pass_definitions`, `pass_usages`, `pass_semantic(_edges)`, `pass_lsp_cross`, `pass_k8s`, `pass_infrascan`, `pass_tests`, `pass_decorator_tags`, `pass_complexity`, `pass_similarity`, `pass_githistory`, `pass_configlink`. Also `artifact.c` (export/import compressed graph artifacts).

GRAPH MODEL & STORAGE
- `src/graph_buffer/graph_buffer.c` — in-memory graph buffer: `cbm_gbuf_upsert_node()`, `cbm_gbuf_insert_edge()`, find-by-qn/id/label/source-type (community 14, god node `cbm_gbuf_insert_edge`).
- `src/store/store.c` — SQLite-backed persistence: `cbm_store_t` (god node, 98 edges), `cbm_store_open/close/begin/commit`, edge/vector/search queries (`store.c` + edge/search/vector store communities; `sqlite3` is a key bridge node).

QUERY / ANALYSIS FEATURES
- Cypher-like query engine: `cbm_cypher_parse/execute`, lexer/parser (`cbm_lex`, `cbm_parse`), expression eval, pattern/binding matching (communities 26, 33, 47, 51, 52, 98).
- Architecture/impact analysis: `cbm_architecture_info_t` with `arch_clusters/entry_points/hotspots/languages/packages` (community 43), impact/affected analysis (`cbm_build_impact_summary`, community 61), code search (`cbm_store_search`, community 67), semantic/vector search + LSH + MinHash similarity (communities 24, 36, 48, 86, 95, 103), Louvain/Leiden community detection (`cbm_louvain`, `cbm_leiden`, community 90), complexity/AST profiling (`cbm_compute_complexity`, `cbm_ast_profile_compute`).

INFRASTRUCTURE / IaC SCANNING
- Dockerfile/dotenv/shell/compose parsing (`cbm_parse_dockerfile/dotenv/shell_source`, community 60), Kubernetes/Helm/kustomize (`cbm_extract_k8s`, `pass_k8s`, community 53, 80), Terraform/Helm chart types (community 45), compile_commands.json flags (`cbm_parse_compile_commands`, community 89), Cargo manifest parsing (community 79).

FOUNDATION / PLATFORM LAYER (`src/foundation/`)
- Cross-platform compat: `platform.c` (`cbm_safe_getenv`, home/cache dirs), `compat.c`/`compat_fs.c` (`cbm_popen/pclose`, fs ops), `compat_regex.c` (`cbm_regcomp/regexec`), `hash_table.c` (`cbm_ht_*`, community 7), threading/mutex (`cbm_thread_*`, `cbm_mutex_*`, communities 40/41), file watcher.
- Memory management: arena allocator `CBMArena` (`cbm_arena_alloc/init/destroy`, communities 93/104, god node 67 edges), virtual-memory pool (community 96), string interning `CBMInternPool` (community 100), memory budgeting (community 38).
- Utilities: zstd compression, path/string helpers, system-info detection, profiling/diagnostics, git context (`cbm_git_context_*`), ADR parsing.

GOD NODES (core cross-cutting abstractions, from GRAPH_REPORT): `cbm_node_text()` (220), `TSNode` (132), `cbm_type_unknown()` (132), `cbm_store_t` (98), `CBMExtractCtx` (85), `cbm_type_named()` (75), `cbm_registry_lookup_type()` (70), `CBMArena` (67), `cbm_ht_get()` (65), `cbm_gbuf_insert_edge()` (64).

OVERALL FLOW: CLI/MCP/HTTP entry -> discover/index repo -> tree-sitter extraction (`cbm_extract_file` + per-feature extractors) -> LSP type/scope resolution -> pipeline passes (parallel) build graph buffer -> persist to SQLite store -> served back via query engine, search, architecture/impact analysis, and the MCP tools. No import cycles detected.

# Architecture of codebase-memory-mcp-pro

A C codebase implementing an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server that builds and queries a code knowledge graph from any repository. It runs as a JSON-RPC 2.0 server over stdio, parses source via tree-sitter grammars, extracts code entities/relationships, persists them to a SQLite graph store, and exposes graph-analysis tools to AI agents.

## Top-level layout
- src/main.c — entry point (MCP server on stdio by default; also cli/install/update/config subcommands; optional HTTP UI + background watcher threads).
- src/ — the application, split into one subsystem dir per module.
- internal/cbm/ — the parsing/extraction engine + vendored tree-sitter grammars (~230 files, dominated by 156 grammar_*.c language grammars).

## Main subsystems / top-level modules (under src/)
- foundation/ (~38 files) — base utilities: allocators (arena, slab_alloc, vmem, mem/mimalloc), hash_table, dyn_array, str_intern/str_util, log, diagnostics, profile, platform/compat/compat_thread/compat_fs/compat_regex, yaml, constants.
- pipeline/ (~37 files) — indexing orchestrator. pipeline.c runs a multi-pass index (structure -> definitions -> imports -> calls -> usages -> semantic -> post). 22 pass_*.c passes (pass_definitions, pass_calls, pass_usages, pass_semantic, pass_similarity, pass_tests, pass_githistory, pass_gitdiff, pass_k8s, pass_infrascan, pass_envscan, pass_complexity, pass_cross_repo, pass_lsp_cross, etc.), plus registry.c (function/FQN resolution), fqn.c, worker_pool.c, pass_parallel.c, pipeline_incremental.c.
- mcp/ — MCP server + JSON-RPC 2.0 layer; dispatches the 14 graph tools (mcp.c/mcp.h).
- store/ — opaque SQLite graph store (cbm_store_*); node/edge persistence.
- cypher/ — embedded Cypher query engine (lex/parse/plan/execute a subset of Cypher over the store).
- semantic/ — algorithmic code embeddings combining ~11 signals for SEMANTICALLY_RELATED edges (semantic.c, ast_profile.c).
- simhash/ — MinHash structural fingerprinting for SIMILAR_TO edges (minhash.c).
- discover/ — file discovery, language detection, gitignore matching, user config.
- graph_buffer/ — in-memory graph buffer (cbm_gbuf) accumulating nodes/edges during a pass.
- git/ — git context extraction (git_context.c, libgit2-based).
- traces/ — runtime trace ingestion (ingest_traces tool).
- watcher/ — background thread polling git for changes to trigger reindex.
- ui/ — optional HTTP server for graph visualization (http_server, httpd, layout3d, embedded assets, config).
- cli/ — CLI subcommands, hook augmentation, progress sink.

## Parse/extract engine (under internal/cbm/)
- ts_runtime.c + 156 grammar_*.c — vendored tree-sitter runtime and per-language grammars (~150+ languages: ada, bash, c, cpp, go, python, rust, swift, etc.).
- extract_*.c — language-agnostic AST extraction: extract_defs, extract_calls, extract_imports, extract_usages, extract_type_refs, extract_type_assigns, extract_env_accesses, extract_channels, extract_k8s, extract_semantic, extract_unified.
- lang_specs.c, service_patterns.c, ac.c (Aho-Corasick), helpers.c, preprocessor.h.
- lsp/ + lsp_all.c — LSP integration for type-aware resolution.
- sqlite_writer.c, plus compression backends lz4_store.c / zstd_store.c (compressed .codebase-memory/graph.db.zst artifacts).

## Overall data flow
1. Discover files (language detection + gitignore) -> 2. Parse each file with the matching tree-sitter grammar (internal/cbm) -> 3. Extract defs/calls/imports/usages/types into a graph_buffer -> 4. Pipeline passes resolve edges (imports, calls via registry+LSP, semantic/inheritance, similarity, git history, tests, infra/k8s) -> 5. Write nodes/edges to the SQLite store -> 6. MCP server answers tool calls (search_graph, query_graph via the Cypher engine, trace_path, get_architecture, get_code_snippet, search_code, index_repository, detect_changes, ingest_traces, etc.). A watcher thread auto-reindexes on git changes; an optional HTTP UI visualizes the graph.

## The 14 MCP tools (public surface)
index_repository, search_graph, query_graph, trace_path, get_code_snippet, get_graph_schema, get_architecture, search_code, list_projects, delete_project, index_status, detect_changes, manage_adr, ingest_traces (src/main.c:300-303, mcp.h:5).

Key files: src/main.c:1-549 (entry/orchestration), src/pipeline/pipeline.h:1-14 (pass list), src/mcp/mcp.h:5 ("14 graph analysis tools"), src/store/store.h:1-9, src/cypher/cypher.h:1-12, src/semantic/semantic.h:1-7, src/discover/discover.h:1-12.

results.question: Find the functions that implement near-duplicate / clone detection (minhash/simhash).

answers.answer:
Near-duplicate / clone detection (minhash + LSH) is implemented mainly in **src/simhash/minhash.c**, with the pipeline orchestration in **src/pipeline/pass_semantic_edges.c**.

CORE ALGORITHM — src/simhash/minhash.c
MinHash signature computation:
- cbm_minhash_compute — computes the weighted MinHash signature
- weighted_minhash_update — per-trigram weighted minhash update
- cbm_minhash_jaccard — estimates Jaccard similarity between two signatures
- cbm_minhash_to_hex / cbm_minhash_from_hex — serialize/deserialize signatures
- hash_trigrams — hashes trigrams for the signature
- collect_ast_tokens — gathers AST tokens to feed the signature
- trigram_structural_weight — structural weighting of trigrams
- normalise_node_type, is_normalised_token, is_identifier_type, is_string_type, is_number_type, is_type_annotation — token normalization helpers
- uniq_trig_init / uniq_trig_insert — unique-trigram set helpers

LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing) bucketing / candidate query:
- cbm_lsh_new — create an LSH index
- cbm_lsh_insert — insert a signature into LSH bands/buckets
- cbm_lsh_query / cbm_lsh_query_into — query for near-duplicate candidates
- cbm_lsh_free — free the LSH index
- band_hash — hashes a band of the signature
- bucket_push, result_push — bucket/result list helpers
- seen_set_init / seen_set_insert / seen_set_free — dedup of returned candidates

PIPELINE INTEGRATION — src/pipeline/pass_semantic_edges.c
- decode_minhash — decode stored minhash signatures
- phase5_lsh_build — build the LSH index pass
- phase5c_build_lsh_buckets — populate LSH buckets
- free_lsh_buckets — teardown

Note: there is no separate "simhash" implementation; the module is named simhash/ but implements weighted MinHash + LSH. File:line numbers are not stored in the graph (fn.line is empty); locations are given by file path.

Near-duplicate / clone detection is implemented by the MinHash + LSH (Locality-Sensitive Hashing) module, primarily in src/simhash/minhash.c, driven by the similarity pipeline pass.

Core fingerprinting (MinHash) — src/simhash/minhash.c:
- cbm_minhash_compute() (L249) — computes the MinHash signature
- weighted_minhash_update() (L208) — weighted MinHash signature update
- cbm_minhash_jaccard() (L269) — estimates Jaccard similarity between two signatures
- hash_trigrams() (L221) — hashes token trigrams (shingles)
- collect_ast_tokens() (L142) — gathers AST tokens for shingling
- trigram_structural_weight() (L125) — structural weighting of trigrams
- cbm_minhash_to_hex() (L284) / cbm_minhash_from_hex() (L297) — serialize/deserialize signatures

LSH banding / candidate lookup (the "near-duplicate" search) — src/simhash/minhash.c:
- band_hash() (L344) — hashes LSH bands
- cbm_lsh_new() (L368) / cbm_lsh_insert() (L373) / cbm_lsh_free() (L525) — build/populate/free the LSH index
- cbm_lsh_query() (L453) and cbm_lsh_query_into() (L492) — query the LSH index for near-duplicate candidates
- bucket_push() (L355), result_push() (L437), seen_set_init/insert/free (L407/L413/L431) — bucket/result/dedup helpers

Token normalization helpers (src/simhash/minhash.c): normalise_node_type() (L98), is_identifier_type() (L65), is_string_type() (L74), is_number_type() (L82), is_type_annotation() (L90), is_normalised_token() (L118), uniq_trig_init/insert (L184/L190).

Driver / consumer (similarity pass) — src/pipeline/pass_similarity.c:
- cbm_pipeline_pass_similarity() (L251) — orchestrates the clone-detection pass (builds the LSH index, inserts fingerprints, merges similarity edges)
- sim_query_worker() (L176) — parallel worker issuing LSH queries
- parse_fp_from_props() (L48), collect_fp_entries(), merge_sim_edges()

Note: despite the directory name "simhash", the implemented algorithm is MinHash + LSH banding (no separate simhash function exists); decode_minhash() (src/pipeline/pass_semantic_edges.c:526) decodes stored MinHash fingerprints.

Near-duplicate / clone detection is implemented with MinHash fingerprinting + LSH (locality-sensitive hashing). There is no SimHash; the module dir is named `simhash/` but the algorithm is MinHash (K=64 signatures over normalised AST node-type trigrams, b=32 bands × r=2 rows LSH, Jaccard >= 0.95 threshold).

CORE ALGORITHM — src/simhash/minhash.c (declared in src/simhash/minhash.h):
- cbm_minhash_compute (minhash.c:249) — compute a function body's MinHash fingerprint from its AST.
- collect_ast_tokens (minhash.c:142) — walk AST, collect normalised leaf token types.
- normalise_node_type (minhash.c:98) — map identifiers→I, strings→S, numbers→N, types→T (helpers is_identifier_type:65, is_string_type:74, is_number_type:82, is_type_annotation:90).
- hash_trigrams (minhash.c:221) — build trigrams and fold into the K=64 signature.
- weighted_minhash_update (minhash.c:208) — per-seed weighted MinHash update (xxHash XXH3).
- trigram_structural_weight (minhash.c:125) / is_normalised_token (minhash.c:118) — structural weighting.
- uniq_trig_insert (minhash.c:190) / uniq_trig_init (minhash.c:184) — unique-trigram set.
- cbm_minhash_jaccard (minhash.c:269) — Jaccard similarity (K=64 agreement) between two signatures.
- cbm_minhash_to_hex (minhash.c:284) / cbm_minhash_from_hex (minhash.c:297) — fingerprint hex (de)serialisation.

LSH INDEX (candidate generation) — src/simhash/minhash.c:
- cbm_lsh_new (minhash.c:368), cbm_lsh_insert (minhash.c:373), cbm_lsh_free (minhash.c:525).
- cbm_lsh_query (minhash.c:453) and thread-safe cbm_lsh_query_into (minhash.c:492) — return near-duplicate candidates.
- band_hash (minhash.c:344), bucket_push (minhash.c:355), result_push (minhash.c:437); dedup seen_set_insert (minhash.c:413) / seen_set_init (minhash.c:407) / seen_set_free (minhash.c:431).

PIPELINE PASS (drives detection, emits SIMILAR_TO edges) — src/pipeline/pass_similarity.c:
- cbm_pipeline_pass_similarity (pass_similarity.c:251) — entry point: collect fingerprints, build LSH, query, emit edges.
- collect_fp_entries (pass_similarity.c:95) / parse_fp_from_props (pass_similarity.c:48) — read "fp" hex from Function/Method node props.
- sim_query_worker (pass_similarity.c:176) — parallel query + Jaccard scoring against the 0.95 threshold.
- merge_sim_edges (pass_similarity.c:232) / sim_edge_buf_push (pass_similarity.c:150) — write SIMILAR_TO edges into the graph buffer.

Note: only the public API symbols (cbm_minhash_*, cbm_lsh_*) and the pass entry point are also declared/referenced in src/semantic/* and src/pipeline/pipeline_internal.h; the actual implementations live in the two files above.

results.question: Where is camelCase / identifier tokenization (splitting identifiers into words) implemented?

answers.answer:
camelCase / identifier tokenization (splitting identifiers into words) is implemented primarily in TWO places:

PRIMARY (semantic search tokenizer) — src/semantic/semantic.c:
- `cbm_sem_tokenize(const char *name, char **out, int max_out)` at lines 166-368 — the main tokenizer. Splits a name into tokens by walking each char: `is_token_delim(c)` handles snake_case / dot-separated / non-alnum delimiters, and `is_camel_break(name, i)` handles camelCase boundaries; tokens are lowercased and flushed, then common abbreviations are expanded (err->error, ctx->context, etc.). Declared in src/semantic/semantic.h:93 ("/* Split a name into tokens: camelCase, snake_case, dot.separated. */").
- `is_camel_break(...)` at src/semantic/semantic.c:148-155 — the helper that decides where a camelCase boundary occurs (called by cbm_sem_tokenize).
- (supporting helpers in the same file: `is_token_delim`, `flush_token`.)

SECONDARY (enrichment-pass camelCase splitter) — src/pipeline/pass_enrichment.c:
- `cbm_split_camel_case` at lines 45-72 — dedicated camelCase splitter.
- `cbm_tokenize_decorator(const char *dec, char **out, int max_out)` at lines 100-137 — tokenizes decorator strings, calling cbm_split_camel_case.

OTHER camelCase splitters found:
- `emit_camel_words` at src/pipeline/pass_configures.c:38-62 (used by `cbm_normalize_config_key`, lines 64-97) — splits config keys into camelCase words.
- `camel_should_split` at src/store/store.c:402-416 — camelCase split-point predicate used by the store/FTS layer (SQLite BM25 full-text tokenization).

results.question: Find the arena / bump allocator implementation (allocation from a memory arena).

answers.answer:
The arena / bump allocator is implemented in src/foundation/arena.c (with header src/foundation/arena.h).

Core bump-allocation function:
- cbm_arena_alloc(CBMArena *a, size_t n) — src/foundation/arena.c:58-76. It 8-byte-aligns n, and if the current block has room (a->used + n <= a->block_size) it bumps the pointer: ptr = a->blocks[a->nblocks-1] + a->used; then a->used += n. When the block is full it calls arena_grow() to allocate a new block.

Supporting functions in src/foundation/arena.c (the CBMArena allocator):
- cbm_arena_init — initialize arena
- cbm_arena_init_sized — initialize with a given block size
- arena_grow — allocate a new block when the current one is exhausted (the growth path of the bump allocator)
- cbm_arena_alloc — the bump allocation primitive (above)
- cbm_arena_calloc — zeroed bump allocation
- cbm_arena_strdup / cbm_arena_strndup / cbm_arena_sprintf — string helpers built on the arena
- cbm_arena_reset — reset (free-all/rewind, reusing blocks)
- cbm_arena_destroy — free all blocks
- cbm_arena_total — total bytes allocated

Key types/macros: CBMArena struct; CBM_ARENA_DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE (src/foundation/arena.h); ARENA_ALIGN alignment constant.

(Note: a duplicate copy of the same arena allocator also exists at internal/cbm/arena.h.)

The arena / bump allocator implementation lives in `src/foundation/arena.c` (with a parallel copy at `internal/cbm/arena.c`), backed by the `CBMArena` struct (defined at src/foundation/arena.c:21).

Core implementation in src/foundation/arena.c:
- CBMArena (struct) — src/foundation/arena.c:21
- cbm_arena_init() — src/foundation/arena.c:21
- cbm_arena_init_sized() — src/foundation/arena.c:25
- arena_grow() — src/foundation/arena.c:38 (internal helper that grows the arena's backing memory)
- cbm_arena_alloc() — src/foundation/arena.c:58 (the core bump-allocation routine: hands out memory from the arena)
- cbm_arena_calloc() — src/foundation/arena.c:78
- cbm_arena_strdup() — src/foundation/arena.c:86
- cbm_arena_strndup() — src/foundation/arena.c:98
- cbm_arena_sprintf() — src/foundation/arena.c:110
- cbm_arena_reset() — src/foundation/arena.c:130 (resets the bump pointer for reuse)
- cbm_arena_destroy() — src/foundation/arena.c:149
- cbm_arena_total() — src/foundation/arena.c:156

The primary allocation-from-arena function (the "bump allocator") is cbm_arena_alloc() at src/foundation/arena.c:58. Header: internal/cbm/arena.h. A duplicate implementation exists at internal/cbm/arena.c (e.g. cbm_arena_alloc at L35, arena_grow at L16, CBMArena at L7).

Arena/bump allocator: src/foundation/arena.c (+ arena.h). Header documents it as "Bump allocator with block-based growth"; memory is freed all-at-once via cbm_arena_destroy(), no individual frees by design. Struct CBMArena (arena.h:20-27): blocks[256], block_size (default 64KB = CBM_ARENA_DEFAULT_BLOCK_SIZE), used (the bump-pointer offset), nblocks, block_sizes[], total_alloc. Core bump allocation: cbm_arena_alloc (arena.c:58) — 8-byte aligns n (n = (n+7)&~7), grows block if used+n>block_size, returns blocks[nblocks-1]+used, then bumps used+=n and total_alloc+=n. Other functions in arena.c: cbm_arena_init (21), cbm_arena_init_sized (25), arena_grow (38, static, doubles block_size or uses min_size, mallocs new block), cbm_arena_calloc (78), cbm_arena_strdup (86), cbm_arena_strndup (98), cbm_arena_sprintf (110), cbm_arena_reset (130, keep first block/free the rest), cbm_arena_destroy (149), cbm_arena_total (156). An older duplicate copy lives at internal/cbm/arena.c and internal/cbm/arena.h (the src/foundation version is the "pure C rewrite" restructured from it). Related but distinct allocators in src/foundation/: slab_alloc.c (slab allocator), mem.c, vmem.c.</answer>

results.question: Which code implements Aho-Corasick multi-pattern string matching?

answers.answer:
Aho-Corasick multi-pattern string matching is implemented in **internal/cbm/ac.c** (lines 1-429, module/file scope).

Key functions (in file order):
- queue_init / queue_push / queue_pop / queue_empty / queue_free — BFS queue helpers used for failure-link construction
- ac_build_trie — builds the goto/trie from the pattern set
- ac_build_failure — computes failure (suffix) links via BFS
- ac_shrink_tables — compacts the state tables
- cbm_ac_build — public constructor of the automaton
- cbm_ac_free — destructor
- cbm_ac_scan_bitmask — core scan returning a match bitmask
- get_decomp_buf, cbm_ac_scan_lz4_bitmask, cbm_ac_scan_lz4_batch, cbm_ac_scan_batch — scanning over (LZ4-compressed) buffers / batches
- cbm_ac_num_states, cbm_ac_num_patterns, cbm_ac_table_bytes — introspection accessors

The "corasick"/"multi-pattern" matches resolve only to this file; it is the sole Aho-Corasick implementation in the codebase.

Aho-Corasick multi-pattern string matching is implemented in **internal/cbm/ac.c** (graph community 54).

Core data structure:
- CBMAutomaton (struct) — internal/cbm/ac.c:L72

Build / matching functions (all in internal/cbm/ac.c, all reference CBMAutomaton):
- cbm_ac_build() — constructs the automaton
- ac_build_trie() — builds the trie of patterns
- ac_build_failure() — internal/cbm/ac.c:L102 — builds the failure (goto-failure) links
- ac_shrink_tables() — compacts the transition tables
- cbm_ac_scan_batch() — batch scan
- cbm_ac_scan_bitmask() — bitmask scan
- cbm_ac_scan_lz4_batch() — internal/cbm/ac.c:L298 — LZ4 batch scan
- cbm_ac_scan_lz4_bitmask() — internal/cbm/ac.c:L272 — LZ4 bitmask scan
- cbm_ac_free() — frees the automaton
- cbm_ac_num_patterns(), cbm_ac_num_states(), cbm_ac_table_bytes() — accessors

The classic Aho-Corasick goto + failure + output construction is implemented by ac_build_trie() and ac_build_failure(), with the matching/scan driven by the cbm_ac_scan_* functions over the CBMAutomaton.

results.question: Where is the Cypher query language parsed and executed?

answers.answer:
The Cypher query language is parsed and executed in src/cypher/cypher.c (header: src/cypher/cypher.h). The file's banner describes it as the "Cypher query engine: lexer, parser, planner, executor."

Pipeline / key entry points (all in src/cypher/cypher.c):
- cbm_cypher_execute (cypher.c:4331-4392) — main entry point. Parses then executes: calls cbm_cypher_parse, then execute_single per statement, handles UNION chains and DISTINCT.
- cbm_cypher_parse (cypher.c:2000-2024) — convenience lex+parse. Calls cbm_lex then cbm_parse, returning a cbm_query_t AST.
- Lexer: cbm_lex (+ helpers lex_push, lex_string_literal, lex_try_ident, lex_try_number, lex_single_char, lex_try_two_char, lex_skip_whitespace_comments).
- Parser: cbm_parse (+ ~35 parse_* helpers: parse_node, parse_rel, parse_match_pattern, parse_match_chain, parse_where, parse_return, parse_order_by_clause, parse_condition_expr, etc.).
- Executor: execute_single (+ execute_with_clause, execute_return_clause, execute_return_simple, execute_return_agg, execute_return_star, execute_default_projection, etc.).

Caller / wiring: cbm_cypher_execute is invoked from the MCP layer in src/mcp/mcp.c via handle_query_graph (mcp.c:1665-1742), which is the query_graph tool handler. (mcp.c includes cypher/cypher.h at line 44.)

Cypher is parsed and executed in src/cypher/cypher.c. The public entry point is cbm_cypher_execute() at src/cypher/cypher.c:4331, which calls cbm_cypher_parse() to parse and execute_single() to execute; it is invoked from handle_query_graph() in src/mcp/mcp.c:1665. Parser (recursive-descent) functions in src/cypher/cypher.c include cbm_cypher_parse/cbm_parse (L1825), parser_t (L439), token helpers peek/advance/check/match/expect (L439-465), parse_node (L545), parse_condition_expr (L1009), parse_return_item (L1503), parse_post_where (L1780). Execution functions in src/cypher/cypher.c include execute_single (L4271), execute_return_clause (L4243), execute_with_clause (L3725), eval_condition (L2390), scan_pattern_nodes (L2793), expand_var_length (L2871). Underlying store traversal is in src/store/store.c (cbm_store_bfs L2652, cbm_store_search L2437).</answer>
</invoke>
